goal: Add a session cache that holds up under concurrent load.
constraints: Must sustain the production concurrency SLA without corrupted reads.
Memory overhead must stay within the service budget.

open_questions.question: Confirm the target concurrency SLA — 10k concurrent users is assumed.
Is the expected memory delta of immutable snapshots acceptable in budget?

phases.name: Design the concurrency model
phases.intent: Decide HOW the cache stays correct under concurrency before any code is written. This is the only compounding decision in the feature, so it gets the most capable tier and a human sign-off.

gate.condition: design ADR exists AND status in {approved, feedback-incorporated}
gate.on_fail: re-open phase with reviewer feedback attached

escalation.trigger: options tie on evidence OR cross-cutting impact

tasks.title: Choose concurrency model for the session cache

decision_expected.about: concurrency model for the cache (immutable snapshots vs mutable-with-locks)

done.statement: A design decision is recorded with options, research, and evidence, and has passed the phase gate.

phases.name: Implement the cache
phases.intent: Build the chosen design and prove it under load. Load-bearing implementation, so it stays on the strong tier; the gate is automated verification plus a human PR review where decisions are surfaced.

gate.condition: all tasks done AND no pending-review ADRs above medium confidence
gate.on_fail: return to implementation with review brief

done.statement: Cache passes the concurrent stress test with zero race conditions.

tasks.title: Wire the cache into the request handlers

done.statement: All handlers read through the new cache; integration tests green.

phases.name: Migrate existing call sites
phases.intent: Mechanically move every legacy call site onto the new cache. No design judgment here — cheap tier, tight budget, automated gate.

gate.condition: no references to the legacy cache remain AND build is green
gate.on_fail: list remaining call sites and continue

escalation.trigger: a call site needs non-mechanical judgment

tasks.title: Replace all legacy cache call sites

done.statement: Zero legacy-cache references; full build and lint pass.
